app.title: iCloudPhotonator
app.subtitle: Photo Migration for Apple Photos

log.network_skip_batch: ⏸️ Network not available — batch will be skipped.

log.automation_permission_fatal: ❌ Automation permission for Photos is missing. Import will be stopped.

log.grant_permission:
Please grant the permission in System Settings.

{path}

Progress: {imported}/{total} files imported.

Would you like to resume the import?
dialog.retry_message:
{count} files with errors found.

Would you like to reimport these files?
dialog.permission_message:
iCloudPhotonator needs the Automation permission to send media to Photos.app.

Please allow the app under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Automation to access Photos.app.

Would you like to open System Settings now?
dialog.permission_missing_message:
iCloudPhotonator needs the Automation permission for Photos.

Would you like to open System Settings to grant the permission?
dialog.grant_permission_message:
Please grant the permission in System Settings.

Click OK when you are done.
onboarding.title: Welcome to iCloudPhotonator
onboarding.message:
To import your photos, iCloudPhotonator needs the following permissions:

1️⃣  Automation (Photos App)
     Allows the app to import photos into the Photos app.

2️⃣  Photo Library
     Allows the app to access your photo library.

macOS will ask you for these permissions now.
Please confirm each with "OK".
onboarding.welcome_desc: Easily import your photo and video collections into Apple Photos. iCloudPhotonator handles large libraries with smart batching, duplicate detection, and automatic recovery.
onboarding.permissions_desc: iCloudPhotonator needs permission to control Photos.app in order to import your media files.
onboarding.full_disk_desc: iCloudPhotonator needs Full Disk Access to read the Apple Photos library database. Without it, imports will fail. Add (and enable) iCloudPhotonator under Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access. You may need to relaunch the app after granting access.
onboarding.full_disk_previous_skip: You skipped this step previously — please grant the permission now.
onboarding.ready_desc: Select a source folder and start importing your photos and videos into Apple Photos.
dialog.full_disk_message: iCloudPhotonator could not read the Apple Photos library because Full Disk Access is missing. Enable it under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access and relaunch the app.
error.no_media_files: No media files found in this folder.
error.source_not_accessible: Source folder is not accessible. Please check the path and try again.
error.photos_not_installed: Photos.app is required but could not be found on this system.
error.check_network: Check your network connection and try again.
error.photos_not_responding_friendly: Photos.app is not responding. The app will try to recover automatically.
error.permission_hint: Please open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Automation and grant iCloudPhotonator access to Photos.
error.file_too_large: File too large for staging ({size_mb} MB > {max_mb} MB limit)
error.staging_failed: Staging failed: Network file was not copied locally
error.stuck_interrupted: Import was interrupted by app restart
error.full_disk_access_missing: Full Disk Access is missing: iCloudPhotonator cannot read the Apple Photos library. Enable access in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access, then relaunch the app.
settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: These are recommended defaults that work well for most systems. If Photos.app becomes unstable, reduce batch sizes and increase cooldowns. If your system handles imports well, you can carefully increase speeds.
settings.language_restart_hint: Restart app to apply language change
